feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Diagnosed with colon cancer at 36, she spent her last years helping others with the disease
entry.summary: The daughter of Iranian immigrants, Asal Sayas worked in the White House and Senate, lobbied for AIDS research and became a tireless champion for people with cancer.

entry.title: Longevity bros aren’t thinking enough about reducing chronic disease. But researchers are
entry.summary: Before trying to rewind the clock on aging, the best longevity approach for the most people may be preventing and treating chronic disease, experts say.

entry.title: How eating habits could influence Parkinson’s disease risk, according to experts
entry.summary: Observational research suggests that certain foods may lower or raise Parkinson's disease risk, though experts caution that diet is just one factor among many.

entry.title: NYC hospitals gearing up for nightmarish World Cup diseases as fans descend on metro
entry.summary: Hospitals in New Jersey and New York, along with millions of their medical personnel, have been undergoing intense training for the past two years to deal with the 1.2 million people from around th…

entry.title: Weight loss and hair loss: The growing hair treatment market from GLP-1s
entry.summary: A common side effect of many GLP-1 drugs is hair loss. It's creating a formerly untapped but growing market for hair treatment products.

entry.title: 7 Things Stroke Doctors Say You Should Never, Ever Do
entry.summary: Strokes are a leading cause of death in the United States — but there are ways you can lower your risk.

entry.title: Confirmed cases of measles in DC and Virginia as country faces significant resurgence
entry.summary: Officials with the District’s Department of Health announced a confirmed case of measles in the city on Thursday.

entry.title: Doctors told her to remove her uterus. The real cause of her pain lay elsewhere.
entry.summary: After years of worsening pain, irregular periods and soaring hormone levels, doctors discovered it wasn’t her reproductive organs driving her illness.

entry.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
entry.summary: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.

entry.title: AI finds signs of pancreatic cancer before tumors develop
entry.summary: An artificial intelligence model from the Mayo Clinic detected early signs of possible pancreatic cancer on scans up to two years before patients were diagnosed.

entry.title: Nature vs nurture: How much of our personalities are determined at birth?
entry.summary: Research is revealing surprising new insights about how much our genes do – and don't – make us who we are.

entry.title: DC-area emergency rooms are seeing more patients with tick bites
entry.summary: The weekly rates of emergency room visits due to tick bites are the highest they’ve been since 2017, according to the CDC.

entry.title: Are oats really healthy for everyone? Doctors reveal hidden downsides you should know
entry.summary: Health News: Oats have become the face of “healthy eating.” From quick breakfasts to late-night snacks, they show up everywhere. Nutrition labels praise their fibr.

entry.title: Vitamin D's benefits: New findings on diabetes, IBD, and Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: In this study roundup, Medical News Today compiles the latest evidence on the benefits of Vitamin D, focusing on the key findings from 3 studies on diabetes, IBD, Alzheimer's disease.

entry.title: Renown Children’s Hospital pediatricians advise on GI illnesses amid outbreaks at five WCSD schools
entry.summary: Renown Children’s Hospital pediatric experts are alerting parents and caregivers to an increase in gastrointestinal illness among children in several Reno-area schools.

entry.title: Beetroot juice isn't for everyone: Hidden side effects and why you should avoid it
entry.summary: You've probably heard the hype. Beetroot juice is good for blood pressure, it boosts athletic performance, it's packed with antioxidants. And sure, there's legitimate research backing some of that up. But here's what doesn't get mentioned nearly as much: beet…

entry.title: FDA expands access to a promising drug for one of the worst cancers
entry.summary: More patients will be able to take the pill while it is under review, as the agency cited the dire need for a drug to fight one of the most lethal cancers.

entry.title: Why Is No One Taking Baby Aspirin for Heart Disease Prevention Anymore?
entry.summary: A new report echoes what cardiologists are seeing with patients.

entry.title: New breakfast trend has Americans ditching cereal for soup as experts support it
entry.summary: Americans are now ditching cereal for breakfast, swapping it by starting their days with soup instead.

entry.title: Dane County sees surge in tick-related urgent care visits
entry.summary: MADISON, Wis. -- Tick season is off to a rough start in Dane County, with urgent care visits already doubling last year’s numbers, according to Public Health of Madison and

entry.title: Doctors Say This Should Be Your First Move for Constipation Relief
entry.summary: When you just can’t seem to get things moving, try one of these home remedies to relieve constipation quickly. They are all approved by GI doctors.

entry.title: 6 Simple Ways to Eat More Fiber, According to Gut-Health Experts
entry.summary: Eat more fiber with six easy expert tips for daily gut health and digestion. Learn simple ways to add fiber to your diet, including foods and habits to try.

entry.title: Scientists Uncover Hidden Poison In Edible Mushrooms Tied To Rare ALS Cases
entry.summary: A new study links toxic compounds in false morel mushrooms to a rare cluster of ALS cases, raising urgent questions about food safety and long-term exposure.

entry.title: Amoebas eat man alive over months in puzzling, ultra-rare cautionary tale
entry.summary: Doctors suspect three factors, each unremarkable on its own, contributed to his fate.

entry.title: Immune Cells 'Remember' Obesity Long After Weight Loss, Study Finds
entry.summary: Defeating obesity is rarely easy.

entry.title: ADHD Isn’t Just a Deficit: Study Reveals Powerful Hidden Strengths
entry.summary: Leaning into ADHD strengths like creativity and hyperfocus may be the key to better mental health and a happier life.

entry.title: ‘Pinky time’: The couch-friendly wellness trick everyone’s talking about
entry.summary: A TikTok demonstrating “pinky time” has gone viral with the promise that this simple daily digit exercise can stave off cognitive decline.

entry.title: I’ve been a fertility expert for 40 years — here’s what you need to know
entry.summary: The midwife and fertility pioneer Zita West on her key advice for anyone trying for a baby

entry.title: 1 in 3 Americans Aren't Getting Enough Sleep
entry.summary: CDC reports finds that women have more trouble going to sleep than men

entry.title: The Next Alzheimer’s Breakthrough Will Take More Than Just Science
entry.summary: At WIRED Health, pioneering Alzheimer's researcher John Hardy outlined the stakes—and next steps—of where treatment is headed next.

entry.title: Illuminating research reveals the surprising way people become narcissists — and whether it’s reversible
entry.summary: It seems that a–holes are born, not made.

entry.title: Biohacker Bryan Johnson makes shocking reveal about girlfriend’s vagina: ‘Top 1%’
entry.summary: One genius commenter dubbed Johnson’s post “c–chie carfax.”

entry.title: WDH reports first confirmed case of measles in Wyoming for 2026; Riverton store listed as possible exposure location
entry.summary: (Fremont County, WY) – The Wyoming Department of Health (WDH) has confirmed a case of measles in an adult from Fremont County with unconfirmed vaccination status, according to a release issued by the WDH on May 1. The release states that this is the first rep…

entry.title: Science-backed ways to keep your bones strong as you age
entry.summary: These are the lifestyle habits that promote bone health and can help prevent osteoporosis from developing as you get older.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Stem Cells That Could Regrow Teeth and Bone
entry.summary: Scientists just uncovered the cellular “blueprint” that could one day let us regrow real teeth.

entry.title: This four-move resistance band workout for seniors will improve mobility and build functional strength
entry.summary: Safely mobilize your shoulders and strengthen muscles in your feet and ankles

entry.title: Syphilis making a comeback, health officials warn. CDC map shows where in Colorado
entry.summary: Compared to about a decade ago, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said congenital syphilis cases are 700% higher nationwide.

entry.title: Long Island has a ‘high burden’ for a potentially fatal blood disorder: new study
entry.summary: It’s caused by a mutation in the gene responsible for making hemoglobin, a protein in red blood cells.

entry.title: Cardiologists Reveal 3 Things That Are Quietly Impacting Your Cholesterol
entry.summary: The cholesterol culprits no one talks about (but you should know about).

entry.title: Neil deGrasse Tyson what happens to our bodies after death
entry.summary: Astrophysicist Neil deGrasse Tyson blew viewers’ minds after revealing what happens to our bodies after death in a recent episode of his podcast “Startalk.”

entry.title: What Happens to Your Liver When You Take Turmeric Supplements Every Day
entry.summary: Turmeric is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, but is it bad for your liver? Learn about the risks and benefits of turmeric for liver health,

entry.title: Simple diet change could relieve painful bathroom symptoms, experts say
entry.summary: New AGA guidelines emphasize lifestyle changes like increased fiber intake and less toilet time over creams and quick fixes for hemorrhoid relief.

entry.title: Dear Doctor: Despite medical advances many cancers remain difficult, or nearly impossible to cure
entry.summary: Breast cancer in particular is highly varied in its aggressiveness and can come back after an apparent cure, even after decades.

entry.title: Popular Vitamin D Supplement Has “Previously Unknown” Negative Effect, Study Finds
entry.summary: A new meta-analysis is challenging long-standing assumptions about vitamin D supplements.

entry.title: Popular Vitamin D Supplement Has “Previously Unknown” Negative Effect, Study Finds
entry.summary: A new meta-analysis is challenging long-standing assumptions about vitamin D supplements. New research from researchers at the University of Surrey, the John Innes Centre, and Quadram Institute Bioscience is raising questions about a common choice in suppleme…

entry.title: Do Mangoes cause body heat? What happens when you eat too many Mangoes, doctor explains
entry.summary: Mangoes arrive with summer and instantly take over plates, cravings, and conversations. From breakfast bowls to late-night indulgence, they feel almost impossible to resist. Yet, every season brings the same warning from elders: “Don’t eat too many mangoes, t…

entry.title: Dietitians Say the 30-30-3 Gut Health Routine Can Help Fix Your Digestion
entry.summary: The 30-30-3 can help you get enough protein, fiber, and probiotics in to improve your digestion.

entry.title: Experts Say People Who Stay Fit Into Their 60s and 70s Aren't the Ones With the Best Genetics or Strictest Routines, They're the Ones Who See Movement Differently
entry.summary: Some older adults keep moving for decades, but the reason may be less obvious than discipline, genes or a perfect routine.

entry.title: Syphilis cases in expectant mothers have dramatically risen since the pandemic – here’s what’s driving the trend
entry.summary: First-trimester screening and intervention has the greatest potential to prevent maternal syphilis. But 1 in 4 pregnant people do not have access to prenatal care during the first trimester.

entry.title: Column | America’s obesity problem isn’t about costs. We just love junk food.
entry.summary: Junk food is cheaper than healthful food, but a new study reinforces taste preferences play a bigger role than prices.
